verified_title: Genome-Scale Identification of Legionella pneumophila Effectors Using a Machine Learning Approach
note: Burstein 2009 PLoS Pathog named Ceg14=lpg0437 and validated Dot/Icm translocation by CyaA reporter; locus confirmed in full text. Replaces Patel 2024 prediction atlas.

verified_title: Genome-Scale Identification of Legionella pneumophila Effectors Using a Machine Learning Approach
note: Burstein 2009 named Lem25=lpg2422 and CyaA-validated translocation (locus confirmed in PMC full text). Replaces Patel 2024 catalog.

verified_title: The E Block motif is associated with Legionella pneumophila translocated substrates
note: LOCUS FIX: Huang 2011 (SidC-fusion translocation assay) lists Lem8=Lpg1290, NOT lpg0945 as in answer key. lpg0945 is wrong; Lem8 is lpg1290 (also confirmed by Song 2022 eLife). Translocation primary = Huang 2011.

verified_title: Identification of two Legionella pneumophila effectors that manipulate host phospholipids biosynthesis
note: Viner 2012 identifies LpdA (lpg1888) as an Icm/Dot PLD effector, localized to the bacterial phagosome during infection. Schroeder 2015 (IAI) characterizes it but notes inefficient translocation. Viner 2012 is the better effector-ID primary; fixes the wrong measles-virus DOI.

verified_title: The E Block motif is associated with Legionella pneumophila translocated substrates
note: NAME/LOCUS FIX: Huang 2011 full-text table lists lpg2391=sdbC (paralog of sidB), and MavF=lpg2351. Answer-key 'MavF=lpg2391' is a name collision; lpg2391 is SdbC. Huang 2011 demonstrates SidC-fusion translocation of lpg2391/SdbC. Verify whether benchmark means SdbC (lpg2391) or MavF (lpg2351).

verified_title: A new method to determine in vivo interactomes reveals binding of the Legionella pneumophila effector PieE to multiple rab GTPases
note: Mousnier/Schroeder/Frankel 2014 mBio is the PieE-specific paper; confirms PieE is a Dot/Icm effector translocated into host cells and LCV-localized during infection (=the 'Weber 2014' hint, correct year). LOCUS NOTE: canonical PieE=lpg1969 (Ninio 2009), not lpg1924 as in key.

verified_title: A Legionella pneumophila effector protein encoded in a region of genomic plasticity binds to Dot/Icm-modified vacuoles
note: Ninio/Celli/Roy 2009 establishes Dot/Icm translocation of the Pie genomic-plasticity-region effectors incl PieF=lpg1972 (full-text table). Mount 2024 also calls PieF=Lpg1972 a translocated effector. LOCUS NOTE: canonical PieF=lpg1972, not lpg1959 as in key.

verified_title: Multiple substrates of the Legionella pneumophila Dot/Icm system identified by interbacterial protein transfer
note: Luo & Isberg 2004 PNAS is the Cre/loxP interbacterial-transfer screen that coined SidA-SidL (SidL=lpg2580). NAME-COLLISION CAVEAT: 2025 papers (He, Machtens) reuse 'SidL' for Ceg14/lpg0437 (the ATP/dATPase); confirm which the benchmark means. For SidL=lpg2580, Luo&Isberg 2004 is the translocation primary.

verified_title: Identification of Coxiella burnetii type IV secretion substrates required for intracellular replication and Coxiella-containing vacuole formation
note: Weber 2013 J Bacteriol = TEM-1 beta-lactamase translocation screen (234 candidates, 53 Dot/Icm-dependent substrates) and the source of the Cir(A-D) replication-defective mutant names incl CirD/CBU0937. Matches the suggested PMID 23813730. Replaces the CirB-only Fu 2022 ref.

verified_title: Brucella control of dendritic cell maturation is dependent on the TIR-containing protein Btp1
note: Salcedo 2008 PLoS Pathog is the founding Btp1/TcpB (BMEI1674) characterization, better than the Felix 2014 structure paper. CAVEAT: it shows TLR2/DC-maturation function, NOT a VirB T4SS-translocation assay; TcpB T4SS-dependence is debated and no clean VirB-translocation primary exists for TcpB itself. Best-available primary; flag for manual review.

verified_title: A bipartite signal mediates the transfer of type IV secretion substrates of Bartonella henselae into human cells
note: Schulein 2005 PNAS identifies all Beps (A-G) incl BepF and demonstrates VirB/VirD4-dependent translocation into human endothelial cells via reporter fusions. Matches the Schulein 2005 hint; better primary than the BepE-centric Okujava 2014.

verified_title: A bipartite signal mediates the transfer of type IV secretion substrates of Bartonella henselae into human cells
note: Schulein 2005 PNAS demonstrates VirB/VirD4-dependent translocation of the Bep effectors incl BepG. Matches the Schulein 2005 hint. NOTE: answer key flags UniProt 'BepF protein' label on BH13440 (=BepG locus); name/accession synonymy to verify, but Schulein 2005 covers the whole Bep family.

verified_title: Brucella control of dendritic cell maturation is dependent on the TIR-containing protein Btp1
note: Same as R179: Salcedo 2008 is the founding Btp1/TcpB paper (the B. abortus ortholog BAB1_0279 is the same protein). Shows DC/TLR2 function, not a VirB-translocation assay; T4SS-dependence debated. Best-available primary; flag for manual review.

verified_title: Identification of VceA and VceC, two members of the VjbR regulon that are translocated into macrophages by the Brucella type IV secretion system
note: de Jong 2008 Mol Microbiol uses a beta-lactamase reporter to show VceA and VceC (BAB2_0123) are VirB-dependent translocated T4SS substrates. Exactly the suggested de Jong 2008 Mol Microbiol ref; fixes the wrong Cryptococcus DOI.

verified_title: Amino-terminal maturation of the Bordetella pertussis filamentous haemagglutinin
note: Jacob-Dubuisson/Locht 1996 Mol Microbiol shows FHA/FhaB (BP_RS09365) is efficiently secreted in an FhaC-dependent manner with secretion signals in the N-terminal TPS domain. Canonical FhaB/FhaC two-partner-secretion primary; replaces the prodomain-mechanism 2012 ref.

verified_title: The major phase-variable outer membrane protein of Escherichia coli structurally resembles the immunoglobulin A1 protease class of exported protein and is regulated by a novel mechanism involving Dam and OxyR
note: Henderson & Owen 1999 J Bacteriol establishes Ag43 (agn43/flu, b2000) as an IgA1-protease-class autotransporter: 18-stranded beta-barrel translocator (beta43) + autocatalytic passenger processing. Canonical Ag43 autotransporter secretion primary; replaces the 2022 BBRC chaperone QC paper.

verified_title: Secretion of the Haemophilus influenzae HMW1 and HMW2 adhesins involves a periplasmic intermediate and requires the HMWB and HMWC proteins
note: St Geme & Grass 1998 Mol Microbiol is the founding HMW1A secretion study: HMW1B (TpsB) translocates HMW1A across the OM; without HMW1B, HMW1A is unprocessed/degraded. HMW1A-specific primary (St Geme), better than the HMW1B-focused JBC 2006 ref.

verified_title: Nucleotide sequencing of the Proteus mirabilis calcium-independent hemolysin genes (hpmA and hpmB) reveals sequence similarity with the Serratia marcescens hemolysin genes (shlA and shlB)
note: Uphoff & Welch 1990 J Bacteriol shows HpmB is required for extracellular secretion/activation of HpmA, with N-terminal sequencing of secreted HpmA confirming leader cleavage. Founding HpmA/HpmB TPS secretion primary; better than the 1997 cross-complementation paper.

verified_title: Gene structure and extracellular secretion of Neisseria gonorrhoeae IgA protease
note: Pohlner 1987 Nature is the founding IgA1-protease autotransporter paper: C-terminal helper/beta-domain forms the OM pore for passenger secretion + autoproteolytic release. ORGANISM CAVEAT: this is N. gonorrhoeae; the answer-key row is N. meningitidis MC58 (same enzyme/mechanism). Canonical autotransporter-secretion mechanism primary (Pohlner).

LOCUS MISMATCH: the only T6SS-4 secretion primary (Wang/Shen 2015 PLoS Pathog, 10.1371/journal.ppat.1005020) demonstrates T6SS-4-dependent secretion of YezP = YPK_3549, the ADJACENT gene, NOT YPK_3548. No translocation/secretion primary found for YPK_3548 itself; the cited 2024 PLoS Pathog is regulation-only. UniProt A0A0H3B6D1 is deleted. Likely the benchmark target should be YPK_3549/YezP (10.1371/journal.ppat.1005020); needs manual confirmation of which locus is intended.
